issues.sample: SEPT. 30, 2015 WE BOUGHT A 2015 GMC TERRAIN FROM BUICK GMC OF MAHWAH (NJ).  THERE WAS 88 MILES ON THE ODOMETER.       AT NIGHT (IN DARKNESS) VISIBILITY IS ONLY ACCESSIBLE THRU THE BOTTOM SECTION OF THE WINDSHIELD.  THERE IS A DARK HORIZONTAL LINE ACROSS THE WINDSHIELD AT APPROXIM

TO WHOM IT MAY CONCERN,          YESTERDAY, 3/22/15, MY WIFE AND I WERE DRIVING WHILE CAROL TOOK HER FOOT OFF THE GAS PEDAL AS WE WERE APPROACHING A STOP LIGHT. AT THAT TIME THE CAR STARTED ACCELERATING. IS THIS A NEW PROBLEM OR HAS IT BEEN REPORTED BEFORE?  WHO DO I CONTACT TO I

RADIO STOPPED FUNCTIONING ON AUGUST 26TH.  THERE WAS NO VOLUME, I COULD NOT CHANGE THE STATIONS BY STEERING WHEEL OR TOUCH.  THE BLUETOOTH WOULD NOT WORK.  THE DASH LIGHTS WOULD NOT TURN OFF AND REMAINED ON THE ENTIRE EVENING.  I CALLED GMC SUPPORT AND THEY SAID TO UNPLUG THE BAT

TL* THE CONTACT OWNS A 2015 GMC TERRAIN. WHILE DRIVING 20 MPH AT NIGHT, THE LOW BEAM HEADLIGHTS DID NOT FULLY ILLUMINATE. THE FAILURE OCCURRED WITHOUT WARNING. THE VEHICLE WAS TAKEN TO THE DEALER WHERE IT COULD NOT BE DIAGNOSED OR REPAIRED. THE MANUFACTURER WAS NOTIFIED OF THE FA

2015 GMC TERRAIN.  CONSUMER WRITES IN REGARDS TO SUDDEN LOSS OF ENGINE POWER, ACCELERATOR PEDAL AND THROTTLE FAILURES.  *SMD  THE CONSUMER STATED WHEN SHE TRIED TO ACCELERATE, THE VEHICLE BARELY MOVED. THE CONSUMER TURNED OFF THE VEHICLE AND RE-STARTED IT, WITH THE SAME RESULTS.

narrative.headline: What 2015 Gmc Terrain owners report most
narrative.summary: Across 313 owner complaints to NHTSA, the most reported area is Visibility/Wiper (33% of reports). Some reports involve a fire or crash — read the details below.

issues.sample: SEPT. 30, 2015 WE BOUGHT A 2015 GMC TERRAIN FROM BUICK GMC OF MAHWAH (NJ).  THERE WAS 88 MILES ON THE ODOMETER.       AT NIGHT (IN DARKNESS) VISIBILITY IS ONLY ACCESSIBLE THRU THE BOTTOM SECTION OF THE WINDSHIELD.  THERE IS A DARK HORIZONTAL LINE ACROSS THE WINDSHIELD AT APPROXIM

issues.sample: TL* THE CONTACT OWNS A 2015 GMC TERRAIN. WHILE DRIVING 20 MPH AT NIGHT, THE LOW BEAM HEADLIGHTS DID NOT FULLY ILLUMINATE. THE FAILURE OCCURRED WITHOUT WARNING. THE VEHICLE WAS TAKEN TO THE DEALER WHERE IT COULD NOT BE DIAGNOSED OR REPAIRED. THE MANUFACTURER WAS NOTIFIED OF THE FA
